name: Forbidden dark matter
summary: Thermal-relic dark matter that annihilates into states *heavier* than itself in the early universe, when residual thermal energy allows the energetically-forbidden channel. The Boltzmann-suppressed cross section sigma_eff ~ e^{-Delta m / T} gives an exponential separation between DM mass and weak scale, naturally producing keV-GeV thermal DM. Annihilations turn off at late times because the heavier final states become inaccessible, evading CMB-recombination bounds on energy injection.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces Omega_DM h^2 ~ 0.12 across keV - weak scales with a single small mass splitting Delta m / m_chi ~ few. Generic prediction: DM annihilates into a slightly-heavier mediator (dark photon, dark Higgs); large DM self-interactions and exponentially-suppressed late-time annihilation distinguish the framework from vanilla WIMPs.

touches_benchmarks.note: Late-time annihilation cross-section is exponentially suppressed (vs. early-universe value), so dwarf gamma-ray and CMB-recombination bounds are evaded.
Direct detection depends on the mediator structure; forbidden-DM with dark-photon mediator predicts a narrow band testable at SuperCDMS and SENSEI for sub-GeV masses.
Large DM self-interactions through the heavier mediator can saturate cluster-merger bounds in part of the parameter space.
Forbidden-DM with dark-photon final state predicts kinetic-mixing-driven signals at NA64, BaBar, Belle II.

new_fields: slightly heavier final-state mediator (dark photon / dark Higgs)
introduces.note: DM mass, mass splitting to mediator, coupling. Relic abundance fixes one combination.

history: D'Agnolo and Ruderman (2015), 'Light dark matter from forbidden channels', Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 061301, arXiv:1505.07107. Active sub-GeV-DM research target through 2010s-2020s; cannibal, coannihilation, and forbidden-co-decay variants extend the framework.

references.citation: D'Agnolo and Ruderman, 'Light dark matter from forbidden channels', Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 061301 (2015)
